creator: vermaden@interia.pl
text:
Hi.

First of all I do not expect anything after sending this message, but I would like to just share how WINE stopped working for me.

It does not matter if its emulators/i386-wine or emulators/i386-wine-devel it behaves the same.

Not so long ago WINE was pretty good on FreeBSD - I even wrote about it:
https://vermaden.wordpress.com/2020/10/14/oldschool-gaming-on-freebsd/

About half a year ago it got broken - I thought that it may be temporary - maybe something in package or latest build - but after half a year later and seeing that WINE actually got some love in the 2021Q3 FreeBSD Quarterly Status Report I thought that I would just share that with you.

Of course the article was in 12.2 and now I am on 13.0 but I recently tried 12.3-RC1 and it were exactly the same.

As in the article I was able to play these games with WINE on FreeBSD:
- Heroes of Might and Magic III: Complete 
- Colin McRae Rally 2.0
- Baldur's Gate: Enhanced Edition (+Expansions)

Today its like that:
- Heroes of Might and Magic III: Complete 
--- black screen after launch - nothing happens.

- Colin McRae Rally 2.0
--- I am able to watch/skip two intro movies but then black screen - nothing happens.

- Baldur's Gate: Enhanced Edition (+Expansions)
--- this one works - but has no sound at all - its def

On the wide note FS-UAE (AMIGA emulator) and DOSBOX (MS-DOS emulator) work like a charm still.

Also all 2D applications work well with WINE - like these for example:
- SumatraPDF
- XNView
- WinSCP

I have /proc mounted as pkg-message for the WINE package suggests for the record.

Not sure what I can add here ... ask whatever you need.

If you have some fixes I can also test them.





Below I will attach what WINE displays on the console after starting these games.

% cat scripts/games-colin-mcrae-rally-2.0-window.sh
cd ~/.wine/drive_c/Colin\ McRae\ Rally\ 2.0
wine explorer /desktop=name,800x600 CMR2.exe
xrandr -s 0

% cat scripts/games-baldurs-gate-I-ee-window.sh    
cd ~/.wine/drive_c/BG1EE
wine explorer /desktop=name,800x600 Baldur.exe
xrandr -s 0

% cat scripts/games-heroes-of-might-and-magic-III-window.sh
cd ~/.wine/drive_c/HoMM3
wine explorer /desktop=name,800x600 heroes3.exe
xrandr -s 0




And the outputs now - as usual with WINE it will not be short.
